density.name: mass density
density.description: Mass per unit volume of a substance. A fundamental thermodynamic property that depends on temperature and pressure; its spatial variation underpins buoyancy-driven flows and stratification.

velocity.name: velocity
velocity.description: Rate of displacement of a fluid element or body. In dimensionless analysis it typically represents a characteristic (bulk, free-stream, or mean) speed that sets the advective time scale.

length.name: characteristic length
length.description: A geometric scale representative of the problem: pipe diameter, boundary-layer thickness, chord length, or droplet radius. Its choice determines which physical effects are captured by a given dimensionless group.

dynamic-viscosity.name: dynamic viscosity
dynamic-viscosity.description: Resistance of a fluid to shear deformation. In a Newtonian fluid it relates shear stress linearly to the velocity gradient. It decreases with temperature for liquids and increases for gases.

pressure-difference.name: pressure difference
pressure-difference.description: Difference in static pressure between two points in a flow field. Acts as the driving force for fluid motion in closed conduits and quantifies energy losses due to friction and changes in geometry.

frequency.name: frequency
frequency.description: Number of complete oscillation cycles per unit time. In fluid mechanics it characterises periodic phenomena such as vortex shedding, acoustic resonance, or externally imposed oscillations.

gravitational-acceleration.name: gravitational acceleration
gravitational-acceleration.description: Acceleration imparted by gravity at the Earth's surface (~9.81 m/s²). Sets the restoring force for surface waves and drives buoyancy-induced flows wherever density gradients are present.

convective-heat-transfer-coefficient.name: convective heat transfer coefficient
convective-heat-transfer-coefficient.description: Rate of convective heat transfer per unit surface area per unit temperature difference between a surface and the adjacent fluid. Encapsulates geometry, flow conditions, and fluid properties into a single engineering quantity.

thermal-conductivity.name: thermal conductivity
thermal-conductivity.description: Intrinsic ability of a material to transfer heat by conduction. Relates the conductive heat flux to the local temperature gradient via Fourier's law; generally weakly temperature-dependent for solids, but strongly so for gases and liquids.

surface-tension.name: surface tension
surface-tension.description: Energy per unit interfacial area (equivalently, force per unit contact-line length) arising from the imbalance of intermolecular cohesive forces at a phase boundary. Drives capillary rise, droplet formation, and interface stabilisation.

speed-of-sound.name: speed of sound
speed-of-sound.description: Phase speed of small-amplitude compressional waves in a medium. Determined by the ratio of the isentropic bulk modulus to density; sets the compressibility scale and serves as the reference speed in the Mach number.

diffusivity.name: diffusivity
diffusivity.description: Proportionality constant in Fick's first law, relating the diffusive mass flux of a species to its concentration gradient. Depends strongly on temperature, pressure, and the solute–solvent pair.

mass-transfer-coefficient.name: mass transfer coefficient
mass-transfer-coefficient.description: Convective mass-transfer analogue of the heat-transfer coefficient. Relates the convective species flux to the concentration difference between an interface and the bulk fluid; depends on geometry, flow conditions, and diffusivity.

thermal-diffusivity.name: thermal diffusivity
thermal-diffusivity.description: Ratio of thermal conductivity to volumetric heat capacity; governs the rate at which temperature disturbances propagate through a material. A high value indicates rapid thermal equilibration relative to heat storage.

time.name: time
time.description: Elapsed time, used as the independent variable in transient analyses. Sets the temporal scale against which diffusion, convection, or oscillation rates are compared in unsteady dimensionless groups.
